title: Fetch items of all boards ordered and filtered by updated_at

posts.author: imai (Michio Imai)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I want to fetch items across all boards ordered by updated_at descending, and want to filter those by updated_at. Now the query is not supported, so I have got 3 steps for fetching the results:

  1. Fetch list of all boards from monday v2 API
  2. In my app, the list is reordered and filtered by updated_at
  3. Fetch list of activity_logs filtered by the boards list, “from” fields, and “to” fields (because items cannot be ordered or filtered by updated_at)

Those queries are a little bit expensive… Like “newest_first” argument (https://monday.com/developers/v2#queries-section-items), if I could choice “updated_first” or so, there’s nothing that could make me happier than this!!!

Thanks,

posts.author: Yoav_Amir (Yoav Amir)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @imai , as you said, currently we are not supporting filtering by ‘updated_first’.
We have this feature in our backlog and planning to do it, but i can’t commit on a timeline currently.

Yoav.

posts.author: imai (Michio Imai)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Yoav, thank you for your rapid reply. It’s more than enough. I wait the feature!

posts.author: peppinob (Giuseppe)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @imai, you could consider to create an automation like:

“when column changes, set date to today”

and than search items by date column value with exact matching through API.

The automation will let you be also more selective in the kind of change you are interested. You could even create more than one automation like the above one, if you are interested in specific columns changes.

As alternative, you could consider to send a webhook when “any column changes” and “when any update is created” to keep track of every change in the board.
